title: Job Openings at the Label Store
content:
The Label Store is a dynamic retail business that caters to a diverse range of customer needs. In addition to clothing and accessories, they also offer a carefully curated selection of groceries. With an emphasis on high-quality and healthy products, The Label Store's grocery section is a favorite among health-conscious shoppers. From fresh produce to pantry staples, The Label Store ensures that all of their products meet strict quality standards. With a dedication to offering an unparalleled shopping experience, The Label Store has become a one-stop-shop for customers looking for fashion-forward clothing, stylish accessories, and wholesome groceries.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:
     

            
    



1.) Deputy Head, Human Resources

Job Location: Surulere, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Support the Head of Human Resources in developing and implementing HR strategies, policies, and initiatives aligned with the organization's business objectives.
	Oversee recruitment, selection, onboarding, and workforce planning to ensure the organization attracts and retains high-quality talent.
	Coordinate performance management processes and provide guidance to managers on employee performance improvement initiatives.
	Lead employee relations activities, including grievance handling, disciplinary procedures, conflict resolution, and investigations in accordance with company policies and labour laws.
	Monitor staff attendance, productivity, and compliance with organizational policies across all branches.
	Coordinate learning and development initiatives to improve employee competence, leadership capability, and overall organizational performance.
	Support succession planning, talent management, and leadership development initiatives.
	Ensure compliance with all statutory employment regulations and internal HR policies.
	Prepare periodic HR reports, workforce analytics, and strategic recommendations for Executive Management.
	 Work closely with Operations, Finance, Audit, and other departments to support business growth and operational excellence.
	Drive employee engagement initiatives that promote a positive work culture and improve staff retention.
	Supervise the Human Resources team and provide coaching, mentoring, and performance feedback to ensure high departmental effectiveness.
	Perform any other HR-related responsibilities as may be assigned by Executive Management.


Requirements


	Minimum of 8–10 years of cognate experience in Human Resources, with at least 5 years in a senior HR management position within the retail, FMCG, banking, hospitality, or any other structured organization.
	Minimum of B.Sc./HND in Human Resources Management, Industrial Relations, Business Administration, Psychology, or any related discipline.
	Possession of relevant professional certifications such as CIPM, SHRM, HRCI, or CIPD will be a strong advantage.
	Strong knowledge of Nigerian Labour Laws, HR best practices, and employment regulations.
	Demonstrated experience in recruitment and selection, performance management, employee engagement, compensation and benefits administration, disciplinary procedures, and organizational development.
	Excellent leadership, coaching, conflict resolution, and people management skills.
	Strong analytical, problem-solving, communication, negotiation, and interpersonal skills.
	High level of integrity, confidentiality, and professionalism.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications and Human Resource Information Systems (HRIS).


Salary
N300,000 - N400,000 per month.

Application Closing Date: 24th July, 2026.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their résumé to: [email protected] or [email protected]  using the Job Position as the subject of the email. 

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

 

 

2.) Retail Supervisor

Job Location: Surulere, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a proactive and experienced Mall Supervisor to oversee daily store operations, coordinate staff activities, ensure excellent customer service, and maintain smooth operational standards within the mall.
	The ideal candidate must possess strong leadership, communication, and problem-solving skills.
	The candidate must reside within Surulere and Environment


KeyResponsibilities


	Supervise daily activities of staff within assigned sections.
	Ensure proper customer service and operational efficiency.
	Monitor staff attendance, punctuality, and performance.
	Ensure cleanliness, orderliness, and compliance with company standards.
	Handle customer complaints and escalate issues where necessary.
	Monitor stock movement and report discrepancies when observed.
	Prepare daily operational and sales reports.
	Ensure safety and security procedures are properly followed.
	Coordinate with management on operational matters and staff supervision.


Requirements


	Minimum of OND / HND / B.Sc in Business Administration or related fields
	Previous supervisory experience in retail, supermarket, or mall operations is an added advantage.
	Strong leadership and team management skills.
	Good communication and reporting abilities.
	Ability to work under pressure and manage multiple tasks effectively.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office is an added advantage.


Application Closing Date: 9th July, 2026. 

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their updated CV and application to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email. 

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

 

 

3.) Storekeeper

Job Location: Surulere, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Store Keeper will be responsible for the effective management of inventory, storage facilities, stock movement, and record keeping within the supermarket.
	The ideal candidate must possess strong organizational skills, maintain accurate inventory records, and ensure that stock is received, stored, and issued efficiently while minimizing losses and discrepancies.


Key Responsibilities


	Receive, inspect, and verify all incoming goods against purchase orders, invoices, and delivery notes.
	Ensure proper documentation and recording of all stock received and issued.
	Maintain accurate inventory records using manual and computerized systems.
	Monitor stock levels and promptly report low stock, overstocking, or discrepancies to management.
	Organize and arrange products in the store according to established standards and safety procedures.
	Conduct daily, weekly, and monthly stock counts and inventory reconciliation exercises.
	Investigate inventory variances and provide reports on stock shortages, damages, and losses.
	Ensure proper labeling, categorization, and storage of all products.
	Coordinate with procurement, warehouse, and sales teams to ensure adequate stock availability.
	Maintain cleanliness, orderliness, and security of the store and storage areas.
	Monitor product expiry dates and ensure timely removal or reporting of expired and damaged goods.
	Prepare and submit periodic inventory, stock movement, and variance reports.
	Ensure compliance with company policies, inventory control procedures, and health and safety regulations.
	Support management during stock audits and operational reviews.


Qualifications and Requirements


	Minimum of a Bachelor's Degree (B.Sc.) in Business Administration, Supply Chain Management, Logistics, Accounting, or a related field.
	Minimum of 2 years of experience in inventory management, store keeping, warehouse operations, or a similar role.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications, particularly Excel.
	Experience using inventory management software will be an added advantage.
	Strong numerical and analytical skills.
	Excellent record-keeping and documentation abilities.
	High level of integrity, accountability, and attention to detail.
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
	Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines.


Competencies:


	Inventory Control and Management
	Stock Reconciliation
	Record Keeping and Documentation
	Attention to Detail
	Time Management
	Problem Solving
	Teamwork and Collaboration
	Organizational Skills.


Salary & Benefits


	N100,000 - N120,000 Monthly.
	Competitive salary package.
	Career growth and development opportunities.
	Professional and supportive work environment.
	Training and capacity-building opportunities.


Application Closing Date: 20th July, 2026.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their updated CV and application to: [email protected] using "Application for Store Keeper Position" as the subject of the email.

     

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Payroll Expert",
  "description": "Coca-Cola HBC (Coca-Cola Hellenic Bottling Company) is a bottling partner of The Coca-Cola Company. Coca-Cola HBC is headquartered in Zug, Switzerland and has a premium listing on the London Stock Exchange and secondary listing on the Athens Exchange. The Coca-Cola Company is the world&#39;s largest beverage company and operates in more than 200 countries.

Life at Coca-Cola HBC is fast-paced and challenging, with fair rewards and exciting learning opportunities. We promote an inclusive workplace, value diverse views and opinions and always appoint the best person for the job, ensuring equality in our shortlists and appointments. Take up the challenge. Join us!

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Payroll Expert

Job Location: Lagos
Job Type: Full-time

About the Role


	The Payroll Associate is responsible for supporting the accurate, timely, and compliant processing of employee payroll across the organization.
	The role ensures payroll transactions, statutory remittances, reporting, and related financial activities are executed in accordance with company policies, regulatory requirements, and established service standards.
	The Payroll Associate collaborates with internal stakeholders, external partners, and statutory agencies to maintain payroll integrity, confidentiality, and operational efficiency.


Your Key Responsibilities


	Process monthly payroll activities including salaries, allowances, deductions, loans, and statutory contributions.
	Ensure timely and accurate salary payments to all employees.
	Prepare and analyze monthly payroll variance reports.
	Post payroll accruals and actuals accurately within established timelines.
	Conduct monthly payroll validation checks, including negative net pay reviews and payroll reconciliations.
	Ensure compliance with statutory deductions and remittances including PAYE, Pension, NHF, ITF, and NSITF.
	Support internal and external audits by providing payroll documentation and responding to audit queries.
	Coordinate with plant locations, HR teams, and Finance teams on payroll-related matters.
	Liaise with banking partners on employee scheme loans and amortization schedules.
	Support time and attendance compliance in partnership with HR Business Partners and location managers.
	Maintain payroll records and documentation in accordance with company standards and regulatory requirements.
	Ensure exceptional service delivery and employee satisfaction in responding to payroll-related enquiries.
	Support the Rewards team with other compensation and benefits activities as assigned.


Requirements


	HND or University Degree in Accounting, Finance, Human Resources, Business Administration, or a related discipline.
	2&ndash;4 years of experience in Payroll Administration, Accounting, Compensation &amp; Benefits, or a related field.
	Experience within the FMCG industry is an added advantage.
	Working knowledge of SAP or other ERP systems.
	Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel and data analysis.
	Good understanding of Nigerian payroll legislation and statutory requirements.
	Experience in payroll reporting, reconciliations, and financial analysis.
	Familiarity with payroll controls, compliance, and audit requirements.
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
	Strong stakeholder management and collaboration skills.
	High level of integrity and ability to handle confidential information.
	Attention to detail and high level of accuracy.

Payroll Expert Job at Coca-Cola Hellenic Bottling Company




        
    

        
        Coca-Cola HBC (Coca-Cola Hellenic Bottling Company) is a bottling partner of The Coca-Cola Company. Coca-Cola HBC is headquartered in Zug, Switzerland and has a premium listing on the London Stock Exchange and secondary listing on the Athens Exchange. The Coca-Cola Company is the world's largest beverage company and operates in more than 200 countries.

Life at Coca-Cola HBC is fast-paced and challenging, with fair rewards and exciting learning opportunities. We promote an inclusive workplace, value diverse views and opinions and always appoint the best person for the job, ensuring equality in our shortlists and appointments. Take up the challenge. Join us!

We are recruiting to fill the position below:
     

            
    



Job Position: Payroll Expert

Job Location: Lagos
Job Type: Full-time

About the Role


	The Payroll Associate is responsible for supporting the accurate, timely, and compliant processing of employee payroll across the organization.
	The role ensures payroll transactions, statutory remittances, reporting, and related financial activities are executed in accordance with company policies, regulatory requirements, and established service standards.
	The Payroll Associate collaborates with internal stakeholders, external partners, and statutory agencies to maintain payroll integrity, confidentiality, and operational efficiency.


Your Key Responsibilities


	Process monthly payroll activities including salaries, allowances, deductions, loans, and statutory contributions.
	Ensure timely and accurate salary payments to all employees.
	Prepare and analyze monthly payroll variance reports.
	Post payroll accruals and actuals accurately within established timelines.
	Conduct monthly payroll validation checks, including negative net pay reviews and payroll reconciliations.
	Ensure compliance with statutory deductions and remittances including PAYE, Pension, NHF, ITF, and NSITF.
	Support internal and external audits by providing payroll documentation and responding to audit queries.
	Coordinate with plant locations, HR teams, and Finance teams on payroll-related matters.
	Liaise with banking partners on employee scheme loans and amortization schedules.
	Support time and attendance compliance in partnership with HR Business Partners and location managers.
	Maintain payroll records and documentation in accordance with company standards and regulatory requirements.
	Ensure exceptional service delivery and employee satisfaction in responding to payroll-related enquiries.
	Support the Rewards team with other compensation and benefits activities as assigned.


Requirements


	HND or University Degree in Accounting, Finance, Human Resources, Business Administration, or a related discipline.
	2–4 years of experience in Payroll Administration, Accounting, Compensation & Benefits, or a related field.
	Experience within the FMCG industry is an added advantage.
	Working knowledge of SAP or other ERP systems.
	Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel and data analysis.
	Good understanding of Nigerian payroll legislation and statutory requirements.
	Experience in payroll reporting, reconciliations, and financial analysis.
	Familiarity with payroll controls, compliance, and audit requirements.
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
	Strong stakeholder management and collaboration skills.
	High level of integrity and ability to handle confidential information.
	Attention to detail and high level of accuracy.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

Key Responsibilities

	Partner with department heads to support workforce planning and talent development.

	Strong knowledge of Nigerian labour laws and HR best practices.
	Experience using HRIS and proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite.
	Excellent communication, interpersonal, organizational, and stakeholder management skills.
	Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced and technology-driven environment.


Salary

HR Officer Job at Workforce Group




        
    

        
        Workforce Group - Our client, a leading IT Company, is seeking a proactive and experienced candidate to support its human resources function in the capacity below:

 Job Position: HR Officer
Job Location: Lekki Phase 1, Lagos 
Employment Type: Contract 

Job Summary


	The ideal candidate will drive recruitment, employee engagement, performance management, and HR operations while fostering a positive workplace culture that supports business growth.


Key Responsibilities
     

            
    




	Manage end-to-end recruitment, onboarding, and employee lifecycle processes.
	Support the implementation of HR policies, procedures, and best practices.
	Coordinate performance management, employee engagement, and learning & development initiatives.
	Maintain accurate employee records and HR documentation.
	Handle employee relations matters and support conflict resolution processes.
	Ensure compliance with Nigerian labour laws and company policies.
	Assist with payroll, benefits administration, and statutory HR processes.
	Prepare HR reports, workforce analytics, and other HR-related documentation.
	Partner with department heads to support workforce planning and talent development.
	Promote a positive, collaborative, and high-performance  work environment.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree in Human Resource Management, Business Administration, Industrial Relations, or a related discipline.
	3–5 years of experience in Human Resources, with hands-on exposure to recruitment, employee relations, and HR operations.
	Professional certification such as CIPM, SHRM, or HRCI is an added advantage.
	Strong knowledge of Nigerian labour laws and HR best practices.
	Experience using HRIS and proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite.
	Excellent communication, interpersonal, organizational, and stakeholder management skills.
	Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced and technology-driven environment.


Salary
N250,000 - N300,000 Monthly.    

    

        Click here to Apply

Committed to quality, service excellence, and innovation, we strive to be Nigeria&rsquo;s premier healthcare equipment partner while expanding our footprint to become a leading provider across Africa.

We are&nbsp;&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Front Desk Officer&nbsp;

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers

Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a smart and organised Front Desk Officer to join our Port Harcourt office.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for managing reception duties, attending to visitors, handling phone calls, and providing general administrative support.


Requirements


	Minimum of B.Sc. Degree
	Must be available to resume immediately
	Must be resident in Port Harcourt (Rukpokwu Market Square)
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills
	Proficiency in MS Office tools
	Prior administrative experience
	Computer-savvy and proficient with MS Office tools
	Strong organisational skills and attention to detail
	Discipline and ability to&nbsp;&nbsp;work with minimal supervision.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: laboriahr@gmail.com using &ldquo;Front Desk Officer &ndash; Port Harcourt.&rdquo; as the subject of the email.",

Front Desk Officer Job at Laboria Limited




        
    

        
        Laboria is a trusted provider of high-quality hospital and laboratory equipment, reagents, and consumables, delivering innovative IVD solutions at competitive prices with exceptional customer service. Committed to quality, service excellence, and innovation, we strive to be Nigeria’s premier healthcare equipment partner while expanding our footprint to become a leading provider across Africa.

We are  recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Front Desk Officer 

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers

Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

            
    




	We are seeking a smart and organised Front Desk Officer to join our Port Harcourt office.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for managing reception duties, attending to visitors, handling phone calls, and providing general administrative support.


Requirements


	Minimum of B.Sc. Degree
	Must be available to resume immediately
	Must be resident in Port Harcourt (Rukpokwu Market Square)
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills
	Proficiency in MS Office tools
	Prior administrative experience
	Computer-savvy and proficient with MS Office tools
	Strong organisational skills and attention to detail
	Discipline and ability to  work with minimal supervision.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using “Front Desk Officer – Port Harcourt.” as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Logistics First Line Supervisor II",
  "description": "Habrams Energy offers you engineering, procurement, construction management, operations, and maintenance services for oil &amp; gas projects in the Gulf of Guinea. We provide specialist solutions to international oil companies, marginal field operators, and service companies.

Our&nbsp;&nbsp;work ranges from retrofitting and de-bottlenecking to designing complete facilities &acirc;&euro;&ldquo; with seamless transition from construction to operations.

We are&nbsp;&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:&nbsp;

&nbsp;Job Position: Logistics First Line Supervisor II

Job Location: Lagos

Job Description


	We are seeking a highly experienced Logistics First Line Supervisor II to oversee logistics operations and ensure the safe, reliable, and efficient delivery of logistics services across field operations.


Key Responsibilities


	Supervise logistics operations across assigned operating units, including aviation, marine, shore base, ground transportation, camp, and catering services.
	Oversee all logistics operations at DS1 and DS2/CPF.
	Serve as the on-site contract representative for vehicle lease, field logistics, and infrastructure Operations &amp; Maintenance (O&amp;M) contracts.
	Conduct audits of contractor and company-executed logistics operations, ensuring timely close-out of audit findings.
	Coordinate transportation, lifting, and hoisting support services for operational activities.
	Maintain and oversee the sling register.
	Ensure compliance with company policies, logistics standards, and SSHE requirements.


Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Engineering, Construction Management, or another relevant technical discipline.
	Previous experience in a closely related logistics supervisory role, preferably within the oil and gas industry.
	Strong knowledge of logistics operations, supply chain management, and logistics standards.
	Experience in SSHE management, operations planning, maintenance management, and general marine operations.
	Excellent leadership, communication, interpersonal, and decision-making skills.
	Strong risk management and problem-solving abilities.
	Ability to work effectively under pressure, manage multiple priorities, and meet tight deadlines.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite.
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: hesprojectrecruitment@gmail.com using &quot;Logistics First Line Supervisor II&quot; as the subject of the mail.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.",

Logistics First Line Supervisor II Job at Habrams Energy




        
    

        
        Habrams Energy offers you engineering, procurement, construction management, operations, and maintenance services for oil & gas projects in the Gulf of Guinea. We provide specialist solutions to international oil companies, marginal field operators, and service companies.

Our  work ranges from retrofitting and de-bottlenecking to designing complete facilities â€“ with seamless transition from construction to operations.

We are  recruiting to fill the position below: 

 Job Position: Logistics First Line Supervisor II

Job Location: Lagos

Job Description
     

            
    




	We are seeking a highly experienced Logistics First Line Supervisor II to oversee logistics operations and ensure the safe, reliable, and efficient delivery of logistics services across field operations.


Key Responsibilities


	Supervise logistics operations across assigned operating units, including aviation, marine, shore base, ground transportation, camp, and catering services.
	Oversee all logistics operations at DS1 and DS2/CPF.
	Serve as the on-site contract representative for vehicle lease, field logistics, and infrastructure Operations & Maintenance (O&M) contracts.
	Conduct audits of contractor and company-executed logistics operations, ensuring timely close-out of audit findings.
	Coordinate transportation, lifting, and hoisting support services for operational activities.
	Maintain and oversee the sling register.
	Ensure compliance with company policies, logistics standards, and SSHE requirements.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree in Engineering, Construction Management, or another relevant technical discipline.
	Previous experience in a closely related logistics supervisory role, preferably within the oil and gas industry.
	Strong knowledge of logistics operations, supply chain management, and logistics standards.
	Experience in SSHE management, operations planning, maintenance management, and general marine operations.
	Excellent leadership, communication, interpersonal, and decision-making skills.
	Strong risk management and problem-solving abilities.
	Ability to work effectively under pressure, manage multiple priorities, and meet tight deadlines.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite.
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Logistics First Line Supervisor II" as the subject of the mail.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply
